summary: initial baseline — but KG file was missing in worktree (kg_expansion code path skipped entirely) so the number reflected a degenerate pipeline. Superseded by iter 001b.

Original single-sample baseline. Retired as noise-pathological after iter 003 reruns showed ~80% p95 variance on identical code (samples: 1253/2266/1259/1158/1252). Superseded by iter 003 multi-run baseline (2266ms max-of-5). See research/baseline_v2_aggregate.json.

Phase 1 lint: added time.perf_counter() + try/finally to the three _load_* helpers in RAGChain.__init__. Lint 13→10, drift 0/15. Change is provably latency-neutral (__init__ runs before the timed loop) — yet ALL stages regressed ~20%. Uniform cross-stage regression → GPU/machine-wide noise. This discard is the load-bearing evidence for retiring baseline 001b; the real code change was neutral but the measurement regressed, proving single-sample p95 is unreliable.

NEW settled baseline (max-of-5 runs on same commit). Bundled iter: (1) PROGRAM.md keep-rule relaxed with lint-progress tolerance band, (2) config/settings.py gains 5 additive precision keys (EMBEDDING_PRECISION_QUERY/INGEST, RERANKER_PRECISION, VISUAL_RETRIEVAL_PRECISION, GENERATION_PRECISION) defaulting to fp32, (3) LocalBGEReranker wires RERANKER_PRECISION with fp16/bf16 direct-load path, fp32 path unchanged, (4) all 10 remaining lint failures fixed (rag_chain _do_search/_ranked_from_search_results, query_processor _load_prompt/_get_kg_terms/_load_injection_patterns/_detect_injection/_has_backward_reference/_detect_suppress_memory/_check_llm_available, reranker rerank). Lint 13→0. Per-run samples: run0 p95=1253, run1 p95=2266, run2 p95=1259, run3 p95=1158, run4 p95=1252. Max-of-5 is the conservative settled metric; 4/5 samples clustered 1150-1260ms, run1 at 2266 anchors the baseline. New target: settled_p95 ≤ 1813 (20% from 2266). Protocol change: iter 004+ uses scripts/bench_multirun.sh N=3 per iteration.
